zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Helen De Ross於1505年出生在蘇格蘭雷福福爾霍賀亞德時，她的父親Sir John De Ross Of Halkhead, 2nd Lord De Ross32歲，母親Christiane Edmonstone34歲。她於1521年與Sir John Blair of that Ilk, 10th Lord of Blair在蘇格蘭雷福福爾霍賀亞德結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1540年之後在蘇格蘭艾斯爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Helen De Ross于1505年出生在苏格兰雷福福爾霍賀亞德时，她的父亲Sir John De Ross Of Halkhead, 2nd Lord De Ross32岁，母亲Christiane Edmonstone34岁。她于1521年与Sir John Blair of that Ilk, 10th Lord of Blair在苏格兰雷福福爾霍賀亞德结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1540年之后在苏格兰艾斯爾。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Ross
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from one or other of a number of Scottish and English places called Ross or Roos(e), especially Roose (Lancashire) and Roos (East Yorkshire). The placenames derive from a British ancestor of Welsh rhos ‘moor, heath, plain’, which is the sense of the cognate Gaelic word ros. The Scottish surname has at least three origins. First, the Anglo-Norman family from Roos (East Yorkshire) was introduced to Scotland when Robert of Roos, lord of Wark Castle (Northumberland), married Isabella, an illegitimate daughter of King William the Lion. Second, various families took the name from the province of Ross in northern Scotland and other places of that name. Third, there were Norman families in Scotland by the 13th century who probably derived their name from Rots in Normandy (see 2 below). The descendants of Godfrey de Ros, tenant of the de Morville lords of Cunninghame, were major landholders in Ayrshire, and almost certainly took their name from Rots. The Rose family of Kilravock (Nairnshire) may take their name from either of these three (see Rose ). The lairds of Balnagown adopted the surname Ross after the earldom of Ross (to which they considered themselves rightful heirs) had passed into other hands through the female line.

English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Rots in Calvados (France), probably named with the ancient Germanic element rod ‘clearing’ (compare Rhodes ). This was the original home of a family de Ros, who were established in Kent in 1130 and had major estates in Kent until well into the 13th century.

English: habitational name from Wrose, in Shipley, near Bradford (Yorkshire), with re-spelling of Wr- as R- due to the loss of /w/ before /r/ in early modern English pronunciation. The spelling Wrose is no longer current. The placename derives from Old English wrāse ‘knot, something twisted’, referring to the steep-sided hill on which the settlement stands, with the sense ‘broken or twisting hill’.

variants.name: Ruskin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Tauba, Uzi.
English: from the Middle English personal name Rosce (see Ross 4) + the diminutive suffix -kin.
Americanized form of some similar (like-sounding) Jewish (Ashkenazic) surname, for example   Raskin  .

variants.name: Ros
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Enrique, Luis, Jose, Alberto, Eduardo, Rafael, Elena, Fidel, Joaquin, Manuel, Pablo, Ramon.  Southeast Asian Leang, Phon, Lap, Phan, Pho, Saroeun, Sophal, Sophan, Sophea, Tha, Thang, Yen.
Catalan: nickname for someone with blond hair or a fair complexion, ultimately from Latin russus.
Swedish: ornamental name from ros ‘rose’.

variants.name: Rose
meanings: English, Scottish, French, Walloon, Danish, and German: from the name of the flower, Middle English, Old French, Middle High German rose (from Latin rosa), in various applications. In part, it is a topographic name for someone who lived at a place where wild roses grew, or a topographic or habitational name referring to a house bearing the sign of the rose. It is also found, especially in Europe, as a nickname for a man with a ‘rosy’ complexion (compare 4 below). In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates and similar-sounding names from other languages, e.g. Hungarian Rózsa (see Rozsa ), Slovak Róža and Czech Roza . Compare 6 below and French Larose 2.
English: from the Middle English female personal name Rohese, Roese, later Rose, Royse (ancient Germanic Hrodohaidis, Rothaid, composed of the elements hrōd ‘fame, renown’ + haid(is) ‘kind, sort’).
English and Scottish: variant of   Ross  .

variants.name: Rossell
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Anglo-Norman French personal name Ros(c)el, a diminutive of Rosce (see Ross ). Compare the double diminutive Roscelin, with which Ro(s)cel or Ros(s)el was probably freely interchangeable.
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Rosel in Calvados.
English: variant of   Russell  , with which the name in 1 above was easily confused.

variants.name: Rusling
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): patronymic from the Middle English personal name Rocelin (Old French Roscelin, Rocelin, ancient Germanic Ruozelin), a double diminutive of Rozo (see Ross ).

variants.name: Rohs
meanings: German: variant of   Ross  .

variants.name: Rosse
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Dirk, Joris.
German: topographic name from Middle High German rœsse ‘kiln’, in particular one used for drying hemp and flax or for burning lime.
English and Scottish (Lanarkshire): variant of   Ross  .

nameMeanings.name: Helen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: vernacular form of the name (Greek Hēlēnē) borne in classical legend by a famous beauty, wife of Menelaus, whose seizure by the Trojan prince Paris sparked off the Trojan War. Her name is of uncertain origin; it may be connected with a word meaning ‘ray’ or ‘sunbeam’ compare Greek hēlios ‘sun’. It has sometimes been taken as connected with the Greek word meaning ‘Greek’, Hellēn, but this is doubtful. In the early Christian period the name was borne by the mother of the Emperor Constantine, who is now usually known by the Latin version of her name, Helena. She is credited with having found the True Cross in Jerusalem. She was born in about 248 , probably in Bithynia. However, in medieval England it was believed that she had been born in Britain, which greatly increased the popularity of the name there.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Helen De Ross was born in 1505, in Hawkhead, Renfrewshire, Scotland, her father, Sir John De Ross Of Halkhead, 2nd Lord De Ross, was 32 and her mother, Christiane Edmonstone, was 34. She married Sir John Blair of that Ilk, 10th Lord of Blair in 1521, in Hawkhead, Renfrewshire, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. She died after 1540, in Ayrshire, Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Helen De Ross est née en 1505, à Hawkhead, Renfrewshire, Écosse, son père, Sir John De Ross Of Halkhead, 2nd Lord De Ross, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Christiane Edmonstone, avait 34 ans. Elle a épousé Sir John Blair of that Ilk, 10th Lord of Blair en 1521, à Hawkhead, Renfrewshire, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée après 1540, à Ayrshire, Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lady Helen De Ross 1505 in Hawkhead, Renfrewshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir John De Ross Of Halkhead, 2nd Lord De Ross 32 und ihre Mutter Christiane Edmonstone 34. Sie hat 1521 in Hawkhead, Renfrewshire, Scotland Sir John Blair of that Ilk, 10th Lord of Blair geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb nach 1540 in Ayrshire, Scotland.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Helen De Ross è nata il nel 1505 nel Hawkhead, Renfrewshire, Scotland, sua padre, Sir John De Ross Of Halkhead, 2nd Lord De Ross, aveva 32 anni e sua madre, Christiane Edmonstone, aveva 34 anni. Lei sposato Sir John Blair of that Ilk, 10th Lord of Blair nel 1521, nel Hawkhead, Renfrewshire, Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 1 figlia. Morì dopo il 1540, nel Ayrshire, Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Helen De Rossが1505年にスコットランドレンフリューシュアイアハウクヘドで生まれた時，父Sir John De Ross Of Halkhead, 2nd Lord De Rossは32歳，母Christiane Edmonstoneは34歳であった。1521年にスコットランドレンフリューシュアイアハウクヘドでSir John Blair of that Ilk, 10th Lord of Blairと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1540年以後にスコットランドエイルシュアイアで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Helen De Ross(이)가 1505년 스컷랜드 렌프레w실르 호크헤드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir John De Ross Of Halkhead, 2nd Lord De Ross(은)는 32세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Christiane Edmonstone(은)는 34세였다. 그녀는 1521년 스컷랜드 렌프레w실르 호크헤드에서 Sir John Blair of that Ilk, 10th Lord of Blair(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1540년 이후 스컷랜드 에이르실르에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Helen De Ross nasceu em 1505, em Hawkhead, Renfrewshire, Scotland, seu pai, Sir John De Ross Of Halkhead, 2nd Lord De Ross, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Christiane Edmonstone, tinha 34 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir John Blair of that Ilk, 10th Lord of Blair em 1521, em Hawkhead, Renfrewshire, Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu após 1540, em Ayrshire, Scotland.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Helen De Ross родилась в 1505 г. в местности ховхид, ренфрюшир, Шотландия, её отцу по имени Sir John De Ross Of Halkhead, 2nd Lord De Ross было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Christiane Edmonstone, 34. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir John Blair of that Ilk, 10th Lord of Blair в 1521 г. в местности ховхид, ренфрюшир, Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла после 1540 г. в местности аиршир, Шотландия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lady Helen De Ross nació en 1505, en Hawkhead, Renfrewshire, Scotland, su papá, Sir John De Ross Of Halkhead, 2nd Lord De Ross, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Christiane Edmonstone, tenía 34 años. Se casó con Sir John Blair of that Ilk, 10th Lord of Blair en 1521, en Hawkhead, Renfrewshire, Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 1 hija. Murió después de 1540, en Ayrshire, Scotland.
